item.dc:creator: willCodeForUpvotes

item.title: BEST EVER broccoli cheddar soup (recipe)
item.description:
Love broccoli cheesey goodness? I've been making this for years... Simple, cheesy perfection.
Ingredients: 1/2 onion, chopped 1/2 stick (2oz/57g) butter 1/4 cup (34g) flour 1 cup (110g) carrots, shredded 3 cups (468g) broccoli, chopped 2 cups (16oz/475ml) broth (chicken or veggie) 2 cups (16oz/475ml) half + half* (yes, use it!) 1 tsp (5g) mustard powder 1/2 tsp (2.5g) paprika** 8oz (225g) shredded sharp cheddar cheese Salt and pepper to taste *I have learned that half and half is an American thing. International imgurians use 1 cup (250ml) heavy cream + 1 cup (250ml) whole milk. **I use hot paprika in this- I don't recommend using smoked as it will ruin the flavor. Omit if that's all you have. Final note: carrot haters can substitute shredded potato. I didn't realize there were so many of you out there...
Melt butter in soup pot. Add chopped onion Sautee 2-4 minutes Had to edit with this onion note from @HipCaneDad in the comments: "...the onions must be sweated until translucent and SOFT! Crunchy onions in a soup are a FAILURE!" Don't have failure onions, friends. Thanks for the hilarious note, hipcanedad.
Add 1/4 cup flour to onion and butter. Sautee 1 minute.
It will be a bit clumpy- don't worry, it's fine.
Whisk in broth and half and half 1 cup at a time. A whisk helps break up any clumps of flour for the best texture. YOU MUST USE HALF AND HALF to get the texture of the soup right. This recipe isn't low calorie but it is DELICIOUS. Do it right and enjoy it. If you do (but I know you WON'T) sub for all milk, especially of lower fat varieties, your final consistency will be thinner.
After you have whisked in your broth and milk, add in the shredded carrots and chopped broccoli. Simmer on medium for 5 minutes and then turn heat to medium low.
This needs to simmer 20-30 minutes until the broccoli is tender. Tender, not mush. Stir frequently and adjust heat lower if it starts to bubble a lot. Don't roam far... just stand there and browse imgur like a responsible chef.
Add 1 tsp (5g) mustard powder + 1/2 tsp (2.5g) paprika. These help the cheese flavor really pop. You can make the recipe without these but it's better with them. Sprinkle in a bit of salt and pepper, too.
After 20 minutes, the broccoli has softened and the mixture has thickened nicely. Now its time to add your cheese!
I was extremely busy with work. I cheated and bought 8oz bags of pre-shredded sharp cheddar because it was easy and melts quickly and evenly and I was so tired. Please forgive this egregious error. For those with endless arm muscles and time, a block of Cabot extra sharp cheddar is sublime in this recipe.
Fold in the cheese... just fold it in! You know how! As soon as its incorporated, you can add salt and pepper to your taste preferences and voila- soup is ready!
Yummmmmmmm
I serve mine with a hearty rustic bread Bread Recipe: http://imgur.com/gallery/VPQZIta That's it! Only takes about 35 minutes from start to finish. I could eat this recipe every day and leftovers are fantastic.
Cat tax: all that cooking has tuckered Jerry out. He's napping his best! Jerry would like to politely remind imgurians to hit both the favorite and the updoot... many thanks, friends.... Stay weird, imgur! Imgurians love recipes! I have a few more to share: Dutch Baby Pancake: https://imgur.com/gallery/Dn65mTJ Best Chocolate Chip Cookies: https://imgur.com/gallery/ivP1O35 German Chocolate Cupcakes: https://imgur.com/gallery/T9G9kaD

item.title: Six kids and two adults requires a very large picnic table, so I made a Very Large Picnic Table with eight seats and a lazy susan for outdoor dining.
item.description:
Obligatory finished product first. From the furthest out points, it's about 8'6" across, and the tabletop is about 6' across. The lazy susan (which did not survive the weather and bowed like mad) is 3' across. It is supported with a 4" bearing in the middle and smaller ball casters at four points to support the edges when laden with barbecue foods. I'll be replacing it with a concrete version eventually.
Step one, get hella lumber. This is all kiln dried douglas fir, due to its relatively high strength by weight. Good stuff.
I built a frame that looked a lot like a sailing ship's helm wheel, and screwed it into the top decking from the back, making all the fasteners hidden.
Once I got all of the boards screwed in, I marked out and cut the octagon. I ended up having to trim it all back to make it more even, not sure where I miscalculated, but there were some adjustments made.
After cutting the top to size and shape, i added the edge boards to make it comfortable to sit at.
All rimmed up. I used a 2" forstner bit to drill a hole all the way through the center, to fit an umbrella.
I was considering moving it myself, but it weighed about 400 lbs. I could have rolled it, but that would have chewed up the edges so bad.
So instead I built the bench seats and the base, making sure it was all good and strong and secure. We carried it into place and fetched the top.
I made this poor lady help me carry it around the side of the house. Holding it over our heads to get it over the 6' tall fence was... not easy. But we got it in place, and it is rock solid!
Time to start sealing it. I used a redwood stain, as I had originally wanted to use redwood decking for the top, but that was WAY too expensive (about 4x the price).
The difference is dramatic, and I -really- like the color. Sheds water really well too.
And all done with the stain!
It's missing something.... It needs a lazy susan turntable to eb able to have a decent barbecue without passing plates and whatever all over the place. Don't mind if I do!
Edge glued the cutoff pieces into an appropriately size piece and made a makeshift circle jig.
First pass. I could only go maybe 3/8" deep at a time, and that was pushing it.
That is all the way through, btu the edges were super rough. Time for the roundover bit.
Ah, much better. I epoxied some super thin (0.05") plexiglass to the bottom for the ball casters to ride on, so they wouldn't wear a groove in the bottom under weight, and drilled a hole to allow the installation of the central bearing.
Worked like a charm! until weather made it bow because I didn't get to sealing the top soon enough... We're going to make a 3' round form with a 2" plug in the middle that I will pour a new disk into, then have the kids either press glass beads into the wet concrete, or their handprints and ages at the time. Then I'll seal it with some concrete sealer and reinstall it where the ruined wooden one is. THAT oughtta last. Thanks for reading! Most Viral Edit: I've had a few of y'all ask me for the plans, so here's a link to a sketchup file that has the design I used, to scale, with all the appropriate angles and everything, including a separated duplicate tabletop so you can see how the underpinnings were cut. It is a Sketchup .skp file, so you'll need Sketchup to view it. i used Sketchup 2017, from when it was still free to download and use privately. https://drive.google.com/file/d/19fIlo-iT5o86UWl7TZLAWKVCrWDPKV85/view?usp=sharing
item.dc:creator: brandoncornwell

item.title: Remember; DON'T put this sculpture into the proprietary connection on the Ryobi EZ Clean.
item.description:
This is NOT what you should do to use standard parts with this device. This art piece is not Ryobi approved.
DON'T attach a 1/4inch quick connect adapter.
DON'T add a brass pipe into the device to enhance durablity.
DONT cut off the excess to fit a 1/4 inch QC adapter. For anyone confused, please see previous posts. Don't forget the o-ring! Free download link in comments.
item.dc:creator: AnotherStupidHipster

item.title: Maybe I’m born with it, maybe it’s Maybelline
item.dc:creator: StopLookingImShy

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments!
item.description:
Is it normal to be this strong?
If you're worried about foundation issues in your home, look out for these common indicators: -Sloping or sagging floors, -Visible cracks in ceilings, -Gaps appearing between walls and floors, -Walls beginning to lean or bow, or -Floor cracks appearing.
Foundation cracks are one of the most common issues, but cracks alone do not mean a serious structural problem.
Size matters when it comes to foundation cracks. The picture above is an example of what a concerning crack would look like. The two sides of the gap are pulling away from each other and one side is sinking. We call this “differential settling.”
Make do with what you have.
Sometimes concrete slabs crack due to improper installation procedures. They will need to be inspected by a professional to determine the cause.
"Under Pressure" - Queen
"I'm on the edge" - Lady Gaga
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: Lil dump-a-rooni
item.description:
1. A coworker was shot in a drive-by (collateral damage). They replaced her 2 weeks later.
2. Something has to fix this timeline. Not enough mutual aid for everyone to protest en masse.
3. Ah, but which bean? Bring me a freshly harvested Terrazu.
4. Went and danced in a microburst this week. Don’t forget to water yourself.
5. The bees and flutterbys will thank you.
6. If you say fast enough, guests won’t know either. Well, some might.
7. I need this as a gift for someone.
8. This works, btw.
9.
10.
11.
12.
13.
14. There’s an inordinate number of dog sweaters for some reason.
15. Or this is you because of a crazy day. It’s ok to take a mental health day. Self care is important.
16. Van Webb
17. And you’ve got a great butt
18.
19. The cicadas are full tilt right now.
20. Is it the same dog?
21. It was a simple rule.
22. For those deep reflection highs
23. One of my dogs legit chuffs. He knows.
24. What spells today?
24. Goth Muppets?
25. I would not have lasted 2 weeks.
26. Faster anxiety is one way to clean your house.
27. Such a dark crossover.
28. Aaaah, that’s the good stuff.
29. There’s no need for this now. It isn’t the summer of ‘82.
30. AZ summer. They shoild seriously give you a pair when you buy a car here.
31. Fight until the very end.
32. Stop trying to juggle. Take a break. Enjoy time with friends or family. There’s always another day for the list of things. Time with hoomans is limited.
33. Mycology is a fascinating science.
34. Couldn’t hurt.
35.
36.
37. Or at a friend’s. Or community pool.
38.
39.
40. You can visibly see it.
41.
42.
43. May none of them have been on the top shelf.
44.
45. Kodo tax
46. Sangha tax

item.title: Some of my favorite unmuted reaction GIFs/videos
item.description:
I haven't deployed this one yet.
This one has seen the odd use, though, for only the most dire of stupid comments. I think his matter-of-fact delivery makes this absolutely devastating.
This only works as a reaction gif if you recognize the song, so here it is (including credits for piano dude) for everyone who didn't: http://imgur.com/gallery/07imkJu/comment/2249476105
Of course my secretly most favorite favorites are the handful I clumsily made myself: http://imgur.com/gallery/8ZEMqWs

item.title: An artificial womb that successfully grew lamb fetuses and could help prematurely born babies.
item.dc:creator: dolphinyaso

item.title: Such a simple pleasure
item.description: I spent a little bit extra on some athletic material boxer briefs, and they feel so good. So comfy and breathable. Just a small positive for the day. Most Viral from an underwear post? Truly the content you all crave, I suppose. A few questions answered in the comments: -Yes, there is a “ball pouch”. It is truly amazing, especially in the heat we have right now. -The brand is “Athletic Works” and I got them from Wal Mart. Though it looks like Reeboks and Hanes have similar offerings as well. I just finished up a brew day at work and I’m about to head home, shower, throw on another fresh pair of new underwear, a new shirt I got that is 2 sizes larger than what I normally wear, and lay in some AC while I wait for our D&D session tonight. Maximum comfort. Cheers, and I wish everyone happy underwear hunting.
item.dc:creator: TheJazzButcher

item.title: Y'all this was just the bees knees
item.description:
LOL: Last One Laughing Canada Tom Green, Colin Mochrie, Andrew Phung, Brandon Ash-Mohammed, Debra DiGiovanni, Dave Foley, Jonathan Lajoie, Mae Martin, Caroline Rhea and K. Trevor Wilson stuck in a room together for six hours (broken into 6 episodes) trying to make each other laugh and if they laugh theyre out. Last one gets 100,000 to a charity of their choice, hosted by Jay Baruchel. None of that gives it justice, it was hysterical, I cried laughing
The attempts not to laugh were varied and of differing levels of efficacy
How. How do you not collapse giggling at Colin Mocherie in full shenanigans inches from you. I'd be done for.
The "Oh Fuck" in their eyes is priceless here as Tom Green senses weakness and zeros in
Lookit that line up, glorious
Highly recommend!
item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40
